# Fixture difficulty

*Generated by Claude from the benchmark's experiment runs, and reviewed by the author.*

FULL% per fixture, **aggregated across the current model set** (each model's **default settings**,
plus opus-4.8's `xhigh` reasoning runs and the Google models — added 2026-06-23), broken out
by request tier — `short` (design, one-line name), `verbose` (architectural prose), `spec` (full
wiring). It maps where the corpus is hard: a high `spec` with a near-zero `short` is a *design* gap
(the model can transcribe the wiring but not choose the topology); low across all tiers is a topology
few models build at all. **Difficulty is posture-dependent:** several of the hardest design fixtures
(the Wilson mirrors, wide-swing/cascode mirrors, telescopic cascodes) are recovered once reasoning
is on — opus-4.8 at `xhigh` gets all six Wilsons — so a low `short` is often *default* hardness, not
an absolute ceiling. But a residual core resists even the reasoning-on frontier and gemini-3.1-pro:
`074` (designed by **no** model — 0% on `short`/`verbose`, and only **16% even on `spec`**,
strikingly low for a fully-specified two-transistor circuit), the super-source-followers, and the triple-cascode op-amps. See
[`reasoning-ablation.md`](reasoning-ablation.md) and [`variance.md`](variance.md).

Typical runs per fixture: short n≈112, verbose n≈45, spec n≈83. Sorted by `short` FULL% (hardest design first). Model-set-dependent
— regenerate as the set grows.

## System-prompt impact on the 10 hardest (short tier)

For the hardest design fixtures, the FULL rate across system prompts — which scaffolding (if
any) unlocks the topology. Each cell is `FULL/runs` at that system prompt; **bold** = best for
that fixture. Coverage varies by system prompt, so read counts, not just rates.

| fixture | topology | topology_ports | strict_ports | conventions | formatted | best |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 074_source_follower_pmos_driving_resistor_load | 0/15 | 0/24 | 0/35 | 0/33 | 0/5 | none |
| 122_super_source_follower_nmos_input | 0/15 | **1/24** | 1/35 | 0/33 | 0/5 | topology_ports |
| 119_triple_cascode_opamp_single_ended_nmos | 0/15 | 1/24 | 0/35 | **2/33** | 0/5 | conventions |
| 121_super_source_follower_pmos_input | 0/15 | **4/24** | 0/35 | 0/33 | 0/5 | topology_ports |
| 034_wide_swing_cascode_pmos_mirror_1to1 | 0/15 | 0/24 | 2/35 | **3/33** | 0/5 | conventions |
| 101_two_stage_diff_amp_nmos_no_cmfb_no_comp | 0/15 | 1/24 | 1/35 | **3/33** | 0/5 | conventions |
| 052_folded_cascode_ota_pmos | 0/15 | **2/24** | 2/35 | 2/33 | 0/5 | topology_ports |
| 051_folded_cascode_ota_nmos | 0/15 | **3/24** | 3/35 | 1/33 | 0/5 | topology_ports |
| 086_cascaded_diff_amp_pmos_resistor_loads | 0/15 | 2/24 | 0/35 | **5/33** | 0/5 | conventions |
| 118_triple_cascode_opamp_diff_out_nmos | 0/15 | 2/24 | 2/35 | **3/33** | 0/5 | conventions |

Reading it: a fixture that is `0/n` everywhere except one system prompt shows that scaffolding
carrying the whole result (the topology is buildable but only when the prompt resolves it); a
fixture `0/n` across *all* prompts — these counts now include opus-4.8's `xhigh` reasoning runs and
gemini-3.1-pro — is one **that no model in the set can design** from a one-line request: e.g. `074`,
which resists even the reasoning-on frontier and the corpus leader.
